# Grand Capital — Comprehensive Deep-Dive Research Report

**Report Date:** April 2, 2026  
**Target:** Grand Capital (grandcapital.com / grandcapital.net)  
**Sources:** DayTrading.com (Mar 2026 comprehensive review), TrustPilot, broker official website, Financial Commission (FinaCom) register, batch2-research data  
**Word Count Target:** 3,500+ words with verified real data

---

## 1. COMPANY DEEP DIVE

### Founding & History

Grand Capital was founded in **2006**, making it one of the **oldest brokers** in this research batch with nearly **20 years of continuous operation**. The company has built a substantial footprint in **Eastern European, CIS (Commonwealth of Independent States), African, and Asian markets**, with over **40 office locations** globally.

The Grand Capital Group comprises two main companies, both named **Grand Capital Ltd**, registered in **St Vincent and the Grenadines** and **Seychelles**. The broker focuses on **accessibility** — offering $10 minimum deposits, high leverage up to 1:500, a comprehensive copy trading system (GC Invest), a separate binary options brand (GC Option), and proprietary web-based trading terminals.

Grand Capital serves traders from numerous countries and maintains a particularly strong presence in **Russia, CIS countries, Myanmar, India, Malaysia, Indonesia, Nigeria, Namibia, Kenya, and South Africa**.

### Key Milestones

|------|-----------|
| 2006 | Grand Capital founded |
| 2008 | Expanded to Russian and CIS markets |
| 2012 | Launched copy trading services |
| 2015 | Launched GC Invest/LAMM copy trading platform |
| 2018 | Obtained FSA Seychelles registration |
| 2020 | Expanded instrument range; added crypto CFDs |
| 2022 | Platform enhancements; web terminal improvements |

| 2025 | GC Option binary options brand established |
| 2026 | 20 years of operations; 400+ instruments; 6 account types |

## 2. REGULATION — EVERY LICENSE

### Regulatory Overview

Grand Capital holds registrations with **two offshore regulators** and is a member of **two independent dispute resolution bodies**. Critically, the broker has **no Tier-1 regulatory license** from any major jurisdiction.

### 2.1 FinaCom — Financial Commission (Independent)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Organization** | Financial Commission (FinaCom) |
| **Type** | Independent external dispute resolution body (NOT a government regulator) |
| **Status** | Active member |
| **Compensation Fund** | Up to **$20,000 per client** |
| **Verification** | [FinaCom Register](https://financialcommission.org/check-your-broker/) |

**What FinaCom Provides:**
- External dispute resolution between broker and client
- Compensation fund of up to $20,000 per eligible client if broker defaults
- Independent mediation and arbitration services
- Regular audits of member brokers' execution quality

**What FinaCom Does NOT Provide:**
- Government-backed regulatory oversight
- Mandatory capital requirements equivalent to FCA/ASIC
- License revocation authority equivalent to government regulators

### 2.2 Serenity Financial (Escrow/Dispute Resolution)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Organization** | Serenity Financial |
| **Type** | Blockchain-based escrow and dispute resolution platform |
| **Status** | Member |
| **Function** | Guarantees security of client funds; provides dispute resolution |

Serenity uses blockchain technology to provide escrow services, ensuring that trading conditions are verified independently and disputes can be resolved through a decentralized system.

### 2.3 FSA Seychelles (Offshore/Tier 3)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator** | Financial Services Authority of Seychelles (FSA) |
| **Entity** | Grand Capital Ltd (Seychelles) |
| **Status** | Active |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 3 (Offshore)** |
| **Max Leverage** | Up to 1:500 |

### 2.4 FSC Vanuatu (Offshore/Tier 3)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator** | Vanuatu Financial Services Commission (FSC) |
| **Status** | Active |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 3 (Offshore)** |

### 2.5 FSCA South Africa (Emerging Market/Tier 2)

| Field | Detail |
|-------|--------|
| **Regulator** | Financial Sector Conduct Authority (FSCA) of South Africa |
| **Status** | Active |
| **Regulatory Tier** | **Tier 2 (Emerging Market)** |
| **Compensation** | Financial Services Ombudsman available |

### License Summary Table

| Regulator | Type | Tier | Leverage Cap | Client Compensation |
|-----------|------|------|-------------|-------------------|
| FinaCom | Independent DRB | N/A | N/A | Up to $20,000 |
| Serenity | Blockchain Escrow | N/A | N/A | Escrow protection |
| FSA Seychelles | Government | 3 (Offshore) | 1:500 | None |
| FSC Vanuatu | Government | 3 (Offshore) | 1:500 | None |
| FSCA South Africa | Government | 2 (Emerging) | Varies | Ombudsman |

**Key Concern:** Grand Capital has **zero Tier-1 regulatory licenses**. No FCA (UK), no ASIC (Australia), no CySEC (EU), no BaFin (Germany). The FinaCom membership provides some client protection ($20,000 compensation fund) but is not equivalent to government-backed regulation.

### 4.1 MetaTrader 4 (MT4)

- **Status:** Full support, primary platform for most account types

- **Mobile:** Available on iOS and Android
- **WebTrader:** Yes — browser-based version available without download
- **Charts:** Interactive charts with full customization
- **Order Types:** Market, Limit, Stop, Trailing Stop, pending orders

### 4.2 MetaTrader 5 (MT5)

- **Status:** Full support, available via dedicated MT5 account
- **Features:** 80+ technical indicators, 21 timeframes, depth of market (Level 2), MQL5 for advanced algo trading, multi-currency strategy tester, economic calendar, netting and hedging position systems
- **Mobile:** Available on iOS and Android
- **WebTrader:** Yes
- **Key Advantage:** Superior algorithmic trading capabilities and broader analytical tools

### 4.3 Grand Capital WebTrader

- **Status:** Proprietary web-based trading platform
- **Features:** Browser-based access, no software download required, charting, order management
- **Advantage:** Accessible from any device with internet connection
- **Security:** Data safeguarded using encryption
- **Best For:** Beginners or traders who cannot install desktop software

### 4.4 GC Invest (Copy Trading)

- **Features:**

  - Copying ratio can be set to modulate risk

- **Minimum to Start:** Varies by strategy provider
- **Differentiator:** Multiple subscription models for both providers and copiers

A: The minimum deposit is $1 for Cent accounts, $10 for Standard accounts, $500 for ECN accounts, and $5,000 for Prime accounts. This makes it very accessible for beginners.

**Q: Is Grand Capital regulated?**
A: Grand Capital is a member of FinaCom (Financial Commission), which is a self-regulatory organization, not a government regulator. This provides basic dispute resolution but not the same protections as FCA, ASIC, or CySEC regulation.
